reason: Modifying the mount options for /home changes filesystem behavior. Adding the noexec flag can break user applications or scripts that rely on executing binaries from home directories, and it falls under the policy category of partitioning/filesystem changes which are considered too risky to automate.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; Potential breakage of user scripts or applications stored in /home; Possible impact on services that place executables in user home directories; Requires system reboot or remount to take effect
